headline: Claude 4.6 Opus & New Models Dominate—Agent Era Officially Here

picks.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
picks.tagline: Free tier just got Opus-level reasoning at Sonnet pricing
picks.summary: The new default free model on claude.ai with 1M context window & adaptive thinking. Lowkey the best value play rn—same $3/$15 pricing but way more capable for coding & long docs.
picks.bestFor: coders & writers who were waiting for free tier to get good

picks.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
picks.tagline: Same price as Gemini 3 Pro, literally 2x the reasoning power
picks.summary: New model hitting 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 reasoning benchmark—that's insane for the cost. Zero price increase means you're just getting free performance gains, which fr feels like a flex from Google.
picks.bestFor: teams already in Google Workspace who want reasoning without paying more

picks.name: Windsurf
picks.tagline: The actual agentic IDE everyone's been waiting for
picks.summary: IDEs finally got smart enough to actually think through your code. Multi-file refactors, terminal access, & the whole flow just works. Beats Cursor for repo-wide context rn.
picks.bestFor: engineers tired of context-switching between tools

picks.name: AssemblyAI Universal-Streaming
picks.tagline: ~300ms latency speech-to-text built for voice agents
picks.summary: New model hits lightning-fast speech recognition at $0.15/hour. Perfect for building actual voice experiences without lag. Tbh this is the missing piece for voice agent devs.

watchNext.title: ByteDance Halts Seedance 2.0 Global Launch Amid Hollywood Copyright Disputes
watchNext.why: Video generation tool drama continues—shows how copyright & AI creation is the real bottleneck rn, not the tech itself

watchNext.title: 🎙️ The Next Wave: AI Agents vs GPT-5 — Which Actually Wins in 2026?
watchNext.why: Matt Wolfe & Joe Fier break down why agents are eating models for lunch. Worth 20 mins if you're building anything
